questions.question: Discuss the design goals of an operating system. Differentiate between user goals and system goals.
keyPoints: User goals: convenient, reliable, safe, fast, easy to learn and use
System goals: easy to design/implement/maintain, flexible, efficient, error-free, secure
Goals are often vague and subjective
No unique solution exists -- different OS designs address different goals
User and system goals may sometimes conflict (e.g., security vs convenience)

questions.question: Explain the difference between mechanism and policy with examples.
keyPoints: Example: Timer interrupt (mechanism) vs scheduling algorithm choice (policy)
Separation provides flexibility -- policies can change without modifying mechanisms
Microkernel is a good example of mechanism-policy separation

questions.question: What are the advantages of implementing an OS in a high-level language like C? Why is some assembly still needed?
keyPoints: High-level language advantages: portability, faster development, easier debugging, easier maintenance
UNIX rewrite from assembly to C in 1973 enabled portability
Assembly still needed for: boot code, context switching, interrupt handlers
Modern compilers generate code nearly as efficient as hand-written assembly
